defs: A road or way consisting of one or more parallel series of iron or steel rails, patterned and adjusted to be tracks for the wheels of vehicles, and suitably supported on a bed or substructure.
notes: ☞ The modern railroad is a development and adaptation of the older tramway.

defs: The road, track, etc., with all the lands, buildings, rolling stock, franchises, etc., pertaining to them and constituting one property; as, a certain railroad has been put into the hands of a receiver.
notes: ☞ Railway is the commoner word in England; railroad the commoner word in the United States.
☞ In the following and similar phrases railroad and railway are used interchangeably: --
[In the latter sense railroad, and not railway, was usually used.]
